mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the federal funds rate from elevated levels signals monetary policy pivot toward accommodation. This reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations and lowers refinancing costs for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.5 percentage points within 30 calendar days
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.72% as of mid-March 2020 reflects emergency rate cuts in response to COVID-19 shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% in mid-March 2020, beginning of sharp employment deterioration
regime_hint: labor market shock and recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp in one month, or correlation between monthly UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales volume (RSAFS) reflects real consumer spending and near-term demand conditions. A sustained decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equities reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 735,904M as of mid-January 2020, prior to pandemic lockdown impact
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS declines >2% MoM, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS changes and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. A sharp decline in INDPRO signals supply-side contraction and reduced corporate profitability. When combined with elevated unemployment, this dual supply-demand shock produces strongly negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.79 in mid-January 2020, UNRATE at 4.4% in mid-March 2020
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both INDPRO declines >1.5% MoM and UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the interaction term (INDPRO_change × UNRATE_change) has Rank IC < 0.08 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) reflect forward-looking investment demand and builder confidence. A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening capital formation and reduced future productivity growth. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices long-term growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,246K units in mid-December 2019, prior to pandemic shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where HOUST declines >5% MoM, or Rank IC between HOUST changes and 5-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) is a high-frequency labor market signal reflecting real hiring activity. When PAYEMS growth turns negative or decelerates sharply while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, it signals the Fed is tightening into weakening labor demand. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159,526K in mid-February 2020, FEDFUNDS at 3.72% in mid-February 2020
regime_hint: policy error regime: tightening into labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS declines MoM and FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, or the interaction term (PAYEMS_change × FEDFUNDS_level) has Rank IC < 0.07 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) measured month-over-month reflects real purchasing power erosion. In a regime where inflation is rising while growth is slowing, the Fed faces a policy dilemma. This stagflationary pressure produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real equity valuations compress and policy uncertainty increases.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3% month-over-month while INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 326.03 in mid-February 2020, INDPRO at 101.79 in mid-January 2020
regime_hint: stagflation risk: inflation persistence amid supply shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises >0.3% MoM and INDPRO declines >0.5% MoM, or the interaction term has Rank IC < 0.06 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. A sustained decline in real GDP growth signals recession onset. When combined with rising unemployment, this dual confirmation of demand collapse produces strongly negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings and growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter (annualized rate < -1.0%) AND UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp in the same quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 24,024.96B (Q4 2019), UNRATE at 4.4% in mid-March 2020
regime_hint: confirmed recession: synchronized GDP contraction and labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 contracts >1.0% annualized and UNRATE rises >0.5pp in the same quarter, or the interaction term (GDPC1_growth × UNRATE_change) has Rank IC < 0.10 over any 24-month rolling window.
